perception_themes: AI frames mobile ecosystem as platform-control topic dominated by Apple and Google
Nothing Phone appears only through device reviews, not as ecosystem player
missing_associations: Nothing Phone as ecosystem participant with nothing.community and cross-device line (Phone, Ear, Headphone)
Nothing OS as differentiated Android-based software layer with ecosystem continuity
findings.competitor_association: Apple dominates mobile ecosystem discourse in regulatory, academic, and editorial sources as the defining mobile ecosystem operator.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, Nothing Phone holds 12% visibility at position 3.8 in Mobile Ecosystem; Tavily shows the topic is associated with Apple platform governance rather than handset brands.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources return device reviews rather than ecosystem coverage, while competitor sources discuss Apple ecosystem control at structural levels — consistent with an indexing gap.

perception_themes: AI associates consumer tech innovation with Apple organizational model and product sophistication
Nothing Phone discussed as scrappy challenger with competitive specs, not as innovation leader
missing_associations: Nothing Phone Glyph Interface and transparent design as consumer tech innovation
Carl Pei track record (OnePlus founder) as signal of sustained hardware innovation
findings.competitor_association: Apple cited across HBR, academic PDFs, and institutional sources as the benchmark for consumer tech innovation.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, Nothing Phone holds 10% visibility at position 3.7 in Consumer Tech Innovation; Tavily shows innovation association in public sources is predominantly tied to Apple.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources describe Nothing Phone in performance and value terms rather than innovation-leadership language, while competitor sources frame Apple as the innovation archetype — consistent with a perception gap.

perception_themes: AI associates minimalist hardware with Apple products including iPhone Air and Mac setups
Nothing Phone reviews emphasize stylish and premium design but not the minimalist category label
missing_associations: Nothing Phone transparent-back Glyph-light design as defining hardware minimalism
Nothing brand philosophy (pure instinct design, stripped-back UI) as deliberate minimalist position
findings.competitor_association: Apple owns the minimalist hardware association: iPhone Air reviewed as A Minimalist Dream iPhone, Cult of Mac curates minimalist setups.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, Nothing Phone holds 6% visibility at position 4.1 in Minimalist Hardware despite minimalism being core brand identity; Tavily shows public sources reserve the minimalist label for Apple — an ironic gap.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources use adjacent words (stylish, back to basics) but not minimalist for Nothing Phone, while competitor sources pair minimalist with Apple — consistent with a perception gap where brand intent diverges from retrievable content.

perception_themes: AI associates wireless audio primarily with Apple AirPods across CNET, Amazon, TechRadar
Nothing audio products appear in dedicated reviews but not in general wireless audio queries
missing_associations: Nothing Ear and Headphone as wireless audio contenders with transparent design
Nothing audio as cross-ecosystem alternative (Android-friendly, no Apple lock-in)
findings.competitor_association: Apple AirPods dominate wireless audio sources across CNET, TechRadar, Amazon with multi-generational coverage as the default reference point.
findings.safe_scenario_wording: In Peec data, Nothing Phone holds 1% visibility in Wireless Audio despite Nothing Ear products existing; Tavily shows general wireless audio queries surface Apple AirPods almost exclusively.
findings.rationale: Tavily target sources confirm Nothing audio products exist and are reviewed, but Peec 1% visibility indicates content is not surfacing in AI answers — consistent with a volume/frequency gap where coverage volume is insufficient.
